title: Admin - Search Vendors - Functional Test Plan
author: QA Automation
overview: Functional testing of the Admin Search Vendors feature on the AT/AD Admin Portal. Covers search by vendor name, location, and contact person, dynamic/submit-based result updates, cross-tab search behavior, partial match with minimum 3 characters, no-results messaging, special character handling, and long query graceful handling.

inScope: Search input field visibility and behavior
Dynamic result updates or submit-based search
Search working across all status tabs (Pending, Active, Inactive, Rejected)
Partial match support with minimum 3 characters
Special character handling in search queries
Search field clearing and reset behavior
outOfScope: Vendor list tab functionality (covered by SCRUM-469)
Backend search API implementation and indexing
Advanced filtering or sorting beyond search

relatedStories: SCRUM-469 - View Vendor Lists by Status (search works across tabs)

prerequisites: Admin is signed in to the Admin Portal
Vendors exist across multiple status tabs with known names, locations, and contact persons
At least one vendor per status tab for cross-tab testing

testCases.title: Verify search input field is visible on the vendor list page
testCases.preconditions: Admin is signed in and on the vendor list page
steps: Navigate to the vendor list page
Locate the search input field
Verify the search field has a placeholder or label indicating search capability
expectedResults: Placeholder text indicates searchable attributes (e.g., 'Search by name, location, or contact person')

testCases.title: Verify search by vendor name returns correct results
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page and a vendor with a known name exists
steps: Enter a known vendor name in the search field (at least 3 characters)
Observe the search results
Verify the matching vendor card is displayed
Verify the displayed vendor name matches the search query
expectedResults: Results update dynamically or after submit
Vendor name on the card matches the search query

testCases.title: Verify search by location returns correct results
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page and a vendor with a known location exists
steps: Enter a known vendor location in the search field (at least 3 characters)
Observe the search results
Verify vendor cards with matching location are displayed
Verify the displayed vendor location matches the search query
expectedResults: Vendor cards with matching location are shown
Location on the cards matches the search query

testCases.title: Verify search by contact person returns correct results
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page and a vendor with a known contact person exists
steps: Enter a known contact person name in the search field (at least 3 characters)
Observe the search results
Verify vendor cards with matching contact person are displayed
Verify the displayed contact details match the search query
expectedResults: Vendor cards with matching contact person are shown
Contact details on the cards match the search query

testCases.title: Verify search works on submit (Enter key or search button)
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC2 - Results update dynamically or on submit
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page
steps: Enter a known vendor name in the search field
Press Enter key or click the search button (if present)
Verify results are displayed matching the query

testCases.title: Verify search works across the Pending Approval tab
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC3 - Search works across all tabs
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the Pending Approval tab with pending vendors
steps: Click the Pending Approval tab
Enter a search query matching a known pending vendor
Verify the matching pending vendor is displayed
Verify only pending vendors appear in results
expectedResults: Matching pending vendor card is shown
Results are scoped to the Pending Approval tab
testCases.postconditions: Search works within Pending Approval tab

testCases.title: Verify search works across the Active Partners tab
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC3 - Search works across all tabs
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the Active Partners tab with active vendors
steps: Click the Active Partners tab
Enter a search query matching a known active vendor
Verify the matching active vendor is displayed
Verify only active vendors appear in results
expectedResults: Matching active vendor card is shown
Results are scoped to the Active Partners tab
testCases.postconditions: Search works within Active Partners tab
testCases.failureReason: SSO session dropped during tab switch

testCases.title: Verify search works across the Inactive Partners tab
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC3 - Search works across all tabs
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the Inactive Partners tab with inactive vendors
steps: Click the Inactive Partners tab
Enter a search query matching a known inactive vendor
Verify the matching inactive vendor is displayed
expectedResults: Matching inactive vendor card is shown
testCases.postconditions: Search works within Inactive Partners tab

testCases.title: Verify search works across the Rejected Partners tab
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC3 - Search works across all tabs
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the Rejected Partners tab with rejected vendors
steps: Click the Rejected Partners tab
Enter a search query matching a known rejected vendor
Verify the matching rejected vendor is displayed
expectedResults: Matching rejected vendor card is shown
testCases.postconditions: Search works within Rejected Partners tab

testCases.title: Verify 'No vendors found' message when no results match
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: Edge Case - No matching results → 'No vendors found' message
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page
steps: Enter a search query that does not match any vendor (e.g., 'xyznonexistent123')
Observe the search results area
Verify a 'No vendors found' message is displayed
Verify no vendor cards are shown
expectedResults: 'No vendors found' or similar message is displayed

testCases.title: Verify special characters do not break search
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: Edge Case - Special characters do not break search
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page
steps: Enter special characters in the search field (e.g., '@#$%^&*()')
Observe the search behavior
Verify no errors, crashes, or broken UI
Verify 'No vendors found' message or empty results are shown gracefully
expectedResults: Search processes the input without errors
No JavaScript errors, crashes, or broken layout
'No vendors found' message is shown (or matching results if a vendor name contains special chars)

testCases.title: Verify very long search query is handled gracefully
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: Edge Case - Very long search queries handled gracefully
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page
steps: Enter a very long search query (e.g., 500+ characters)
Observe the search field behavior
Verify the search field handles the input (truncates or accepts)
Verify no errors, crashes, or performance degradation
Verify results show 'No vendors found' or are handled gracefully
expectedResults: Search field handles the input without breaking layout
Input is either accepted or truncated at a reasonable limit

testCases.title: Verify clearing the search field restores the full vendor list
testCases.preconditions: Admin has an active search query with filtered results
steps: Enter a search query and observe filtered results
Clear the search field (backspace or clear button)
Observe the vendor list
Verify all vendors in the current tab are displayed again
expectedResults: All vendors in the current tab are restored
testCases.postconditions: Full vendor list is restored after clearing search

testCases.title: Verify search input field is accessible via keyboard
testCases.preconditions: Admin is on the vendor list page
steps: Use Tab key to navigate to the search input field
Verify the field receives focus with a visible indicator
Type a search query using the keyboard
Press Escape to clear or dismiss (if supported)
Verify the search field has an accessible label or aria-label
expectedResults: Search field is reachable via Tab
Focus indicator is visible on the search field
Escape key behavior is handled (clear or no-op)
Search field has an accessible label for screen readers

testCases.title: Verify complete search vendors flow end-to-end
testCases.acceptanceCriteria: AC1-AC3 and Edge Cases - Complete Flow
testCases.preconditions: Admin is signed in and vendors exist across tabs with known attributes
steps: Navigate to the vendor list page
Verify search field is visible
Search by a known vendor name — verify correct result
Clear search — verify full list restores
Search by a known location — verify correct result
Clear search
Search by a known contact person — verify correct result
Clear search
Switch to a different tab and repeat a search — verify it works
Enter a non-matching query — verify 'No vendors found' message
Enter special characters — verify no errors
expectedResults: Vendor name search returns correct vendor
Full list is restored after clearing
Contact person search returns correct vendor
Search works on the other tab
testCases.postconditions: Full search flow is verified across attributes and tabs

vendorData: Vendors with unique, known names for name search testing
Vendors in different locations for location search testing
Vendors with known contact person names for contact search testing
Vendors across all four status tabs (Pending, Active, Inactive, Rejected)
At least one vendor per tab for cross-tab search verification
searchQueries: 1-character and 2-character strings for minimum threshold testing

assumptions: Admin Portal is accessible and admin sign-in works (SCRUM-442)
Vendor list page with status tabs is available (SCRUM-469)
Search is a single unified input field (not separate fields per attribute)
Search matches against vendor name, location, and contact person simultaneously
Minimum 3 characters required before search triggers
risks: Dynamic vs submit-based search behavior is ambiguous ('dynamically or on submit') — may be one or the other
Cross-tab search behavior is unclear — does search persist when switching tabs or reset?
Search performance with large vendor datasets is not specified
Maximum search query length is not defined
openQuestions: Is the search dynamic (as-you-type) or submit-based (Enter/button), or both?
Does the search query persist when switching between status tabs?
Is there a clear/reset button on the search field?
Is there a maximum character limit for the search input?
Does the search match against all three attributes simultaneously or is there a dropdown to select the attribute?
Note: 'Application ID' was removed from search attributes on 2026-04-14. Confirm this is intentional.
notesFromChangelog: Description updated 2026-02-10: 'min of 3 characters' added to partial match requirement.
Description updated 2026-04-14: 'Application ID' removed from search-supported attributes. Current attributes: Vendor name, Location, Contact person.
